simple_1.question: How many more "discount" gas stations does the Czech Republic have compared to Slovakia?
simple_1.evidence: Czech Republic can be represented as the Country value in gasstations table is 'CZE'; Slovakia can be represented as the Country value in the gasstations table is 'SVK';  Computation of more "discount" gas stations= Total no. of discount gas stations in Czech Republic - Total no. of discount gas stations in Slovakia
simple_1.SQL: SELECT SUM(IIF(Country = 'CZE', 1, 0)) - SUM(IIF(Country = 'SVK', 1, 0)) FROM gasstations WHERE Segment = 'Discount'

simple_2.question: What is the most common bond type?
simple_2.evidence: most common bond type refers MAX(COUNT(bond_type))
simple_2.SQL: SELECT T.bond_type FROM ( SELECT bond_type, COUNT(bond_id) FROM bond GROUP BY bond_type ORDER BY COUNT(bond_id) DESC LIMIT 1 ) AS T

simple_3.question: Please give the name of the race held on the circuits in Germany.
simple_3.evidence: Germany is a name of country;
simple_3.SQL: SELECT DISTINCT T2.name FROM circuits AS T1 INNER JOIN races AS T2 ON T2.circuitID = T1.circuitId WHERE T1.country = 'Germany'

simple_4.question: What is the highest eligible free rate for K-12 students in the schools in Alameda County?
simple_4.evidence: Eligible free rate for K-12 = `Free Meal Count (K-12)` / `Enrollment (K-12)`
simple_4.SQL: SELECT `Free Meal Count (K-12)` / `Enrollment (K-12)` FROM frpm WHERE `County Name` = 'Alameda' ORDER BY (CAST(`Free Meal Count (K-12)` AS REAL) / `Enrollment (K-12)`) DESC LIMIT 1

simple_5.question: How many accounts who have region in Prague are eligible for loans?
simple_5.evidence: A3 contains the data of region
simple_5.SQL: SELECT COUNT(T1.account_id) FROM account AS T1 INNER JOIN loan AS T2 ON T1.account_id = T2.account_id INNER JOIN district AS T3 ON T1.district_id = T3.district_id WHERE T3.A3 = 'Prague'

moderate_1.question: How many matches were held in the Belgium Jupiler League in April, 2009?
moderate_1.evidence: Belgium Jupiler League refers to League.name = 'Belgium Jupiler League'; in April, 2009 refers to SUBSTR(`date`, 1, 7);
moderate_1.SQL: SELECT COUNT(t2.id) FROM League AS t1 INNER JOIN Match AS t2 ON t1.id = t2.league_id WHERE t1.name = 'Belgium Jupiler League' AND SUBSTR(t2.`date`, 1, 7) = '2009-04'

moderate_2.question: Calculate the average number of oxygen atoms in single-bonded molecules.
moderate_2.evidence: single-bonded molecules refers to bond_type = '-' ; average number of oxygen atom = AVG(element = 'o')
moderate_2.SQL: SELECT AVG(oxygen_count) FROM (SELECT T1.molecule_id, COUNT(T1.element) AS oxygen_count FROM atom AS T1 INNER JOIN bond AS T2 ON T1.molecule_id = T2.molecule_id  WHERE T2.bond_type = '-' AND T1.element = 'o'  GROUP BY T1.molecule_id) AS oxygen_counts

moderate_3.question: For the Bahrain Grand Prix in 2007, how many drivers not finished the game?
moderate_3.evidence: Bahrain Grand Prix refers to races.name = 'Bahrain Grand Prix'; drivers who finished the race refers to time is not empty (i.e. time IS NOT NULL);
moderate_3.SQL: SELECT COUNT(T3.driverId) FROM races AS T1 INNER JOIN results AS T2 ON T2.raceId = T1.raceId INNER JOIN drivers AS T3 ON T3.driverId = T2.driverId WHERE T1.year = 2007 AND T1.name = 'Bahrain Grand Prix' AND T2.time IS NULL

moderate_4.question: Give the names of the schools with the percent eligible for free meals in K-12 is more than 0.1 and test takers whose test score is greater than or equal to 1500?
moderate_4.evidence: Percent eligible for free meals = Free Meal Count (K-12) / Total (Enrollment (K-12)
moderate_4.SQL: SELECT T2.`School Name` FROM satscores AS T1 INNER JOIN frpm AS T2 ON T1.cds = T2.CDSCode WHERE CAST(T2.`Free Meal Count (K-12)` AS REAL) / T2.`Enrollment (K-12)` > 0.1 AND T1.NumGE1500 > 0

moderate_5.question: How many male customers who are living in North Bohemia have average salary greater than 8000?
moderate_5.evidence: Male means that gender = 'M'; A3 refers to region; A11 pertains to average salary.
moderate_5.SQL: SELECT COUNT(T1.client_id) FROM client AS T1 INNER JOIN district AS T2 ON T1.district_id = T2.district_id WHERE T1.gender = 'M' AND T2.A3 = 'north Bohemia' AND T2.A11 > 8000

challenging_1.question: Calculate the percentage of players who prefer left foot, who were born between 1987 and 1992.
challenging_1.evidence: players who prefer left foot refers to preferred_foot = 'left'; percentage of players who prefer left foot = DIVIDE(MULTIPLY((SUM(preferred_foot = 'left'), 100)), COUNT(player_fifa_api_id)); born between 1987 and 1992 refers to YEAR(birthday) BETWEEN '1987' AND '1992';
challenging_1.SQL: SELECT CAST(COUNT(CASE WHEN t2.preferred_foot = 'left' THEN t1.id ELSE NULL END) AS REAL) * 100 / COUNT(t1.id) percent FROM Player AS t1 INNER JOIN Player_Attributes AS t2 ON t1.player_api_id = t2.player_api_id WHERE SUBSTR(t1.birthday, 1, 4) BETWEEN '1987' AND '1992'

challenging_2.question: Which element is the least numerous in non-carcinogenic molecules?
challenging_2.evidence: label = '-' means molecules are non-carcinogenic; least numerous refers to MIN(COUNT(element));
challenging_2.SQL: SELECT T.element FROM (SELECT T1.element, COUNT(DISTINCT T1.molecule_id) FROM atom AS T1 INNER JOIN molecule AS T2 ON T1.molecule_id = T2.molecule_id WHERE T2.label = '-' GROUP BY T1.element ORDER BY COUNT(DISTINCT T1.molecule_id) ASC LIMIT 1) t

challenging_3.question: Please calculate the race completion percentage of Japanese drivers from 2007 to 2009.
challenging_3.evidence: from 2007 to 2009 refers to year between 2007 and 2009; race completion refers to time is not null; percentage = Divide(COUNT(DriverID where time is not null and year between 2007 and 2009),Count (DriverID where year between 2007 and 2009))*100;
challenging_3.SQL: SELECT CAST(SUM(IIF(T1.time IS NOT NULL, 1, 0)) AS REAL) * 100 / COUNT(T1.raceId) FROM results AS T1 INNER JOIN races AS T2 on T1.raceId = T2.raceId INNER JOIN drivers AS T3 on T1.driverId = T3.driverId WHERE T3.nationality = 'Japanese' AND T2.year BETWEEN 2007 AND 2009

challenging_4.question: What is the total number of non-chartered schools in the county of Los Angeles with a percent (%) of eligible free meals for grades 1 through 12 that is less than 0.18%?
challenging_4.evidence: non-chartered schools refer to schools whose Charter = 0; K-12 means grades 1 through 12; percent of eligible free rate for K-12 = `Free Meal Count (K-12)` * 100 / `Enrollment (K-12)`
challenging_4.SQL: SELECT COUNT(T2.School) FROM frpm AS T1 INNER JOIN schools AS T2 ON T1.CDSCode = T2.CDSCode WHERE T2.County = 'Los Angeles' AND T2.Charter = 0 AND CAST(T1.`Free Meal Count (K-12)` AS REAL) * 100 / T1.`Enrollment (K-12)` < 0.18

challenging_5.question: For the branch which located in the south Bohemia with biggest number of inhabitants, what is the percentage of the male clients?
challenging_5.evidence: Percentage of the male clients = DIVIDE(COUNT(male clients), COUNT(clients)) * 100; Male refers to gender = 'M', A3 is the region name. A4 contains the information about inhabitants.
challenging_5.SQL: SELECT CAST(SUM(T1.gender = 'M') AS REAL) * 100 / COUNT(T1.client_id) FROM client AS T1 INNER JOIN district AS T2 ON T1.district_id = T2.district_id WHERE T2.A3 = 'south Bohemia' GROUP BY T2.A4 ORDER BY T2.A4 DESC LIMIT 1
